proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Adult Reconstruction Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite hip and knee arthroplasty model linking joint surfaces, soft tissues, and implant interfaces.
Bony pelvic ring anchoring the acetabulum and hip center of rotation.
Hip socket receiving the acetabular component; key for cup positioning and hip stability.
Femoral head, neck, trochanters, and calcar region relevant to THA stem fixation and offset.
Periarticular soft tissue envelope influencing THA approach, stability, and dislocation risk.
Fibrocartilaginous hip rim contributing to suction seal and joint stability.
Primary hip abductor; critical for gait and THA abductor repair integrity.
Deep hip abductor contributing to pelvic stability during gait.
Posterior hip soft tissue sleeve repaired after posterior THA approach.
Anterior hip nerve at risk during anterior approach THA exposure.
Thigh bone bearing femoral condyles and canal for arthroplasty stem fixation.
Proximal tibial plateau and metaphysis receiving tibial baseplate in TKA.
Distal femoral articular surfaces resurfaced during TKA femoral component placement.
Medial and lateral knee stabilizers balanced during TKA soft-tissue releases.
ACL and PCL influencing TKA constraint selection and balancing strategy.
Proximal extensor mechanism linkage at risk in TKA exposure and extensor failure.
Distal extensor mechanism linkage vulnerable to rupture after TKA.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Arthroplasty Implant Concepts Hub

metadata.description: Composite model of reusable THA and TKA implant components and fixation strategies.
TKA femoral resurfacing component restoring flexion-extension geometry.
THA cup restoring acetabular coverage and hip center of rotation.
Bearing insert articulating with femoral head or femoral component.
THA femoral canal implant transmitting load from head to diaphysis.
TKA tibial tray fixed to proximal tibia receiving polyethylene insert.
TKA polyethylene bearing surface articulating with femoral component.
Resurfaced patellar button in select TKA and patellofemoral arthroplasty cases.
PMMA interphase distributing load between implant and bone.
Cementless implant stability achieved through interference fit and bone ingrowth.
PMMA cement securing implant to bone for immediate stability.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Proximal Femur Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite proximal femur model linking head, neck, trochanters, calcar, and hip joint.
Articular femoral head supplied largely by retinacular and circumflex vessels.
Intracapsular segment between femoral head and intertrochanteric region.
Extracapsular metaphyseal zone between neck and subtrochanteric diaphysis.
Medial proximal femur attachment for iliopsoas; key subtrochanteric landmark.
Lateral proximal femur with abductor insertions; intertrochanteric fixation landmark.
Dense medial femoral neck cortical buttress supporting head-neck load transfer.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Medial Femoral Circumflex Artery

metadata.description: Dominant retinacular blood supply to the femoral head; vulnerable in neck fractures.
Posterior hip nerve at risk during proximal femur exposure and reduction maneuvers.
Ball-and-socket articulation between femoral head and acetabulum.
Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Distal femoral metaphysis and condylar region forming the proximal knee.
Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Sesamoid bone within the extensor mechanism at the anterior knee.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage enabling knee extension.
Major knee-region artery at risk in tibial plateau, distal femur, and knee dislocation trauma.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Aseptic Loosening of TKA

metadata.description: Mechanical failure of TKA fixation without infection.
Progressive tibial tray settling indicating loosening.

metadata.claim_text: Exclude infection before diagnosing aseptic TKA loosening.
Board trap: oversimplifying aseptic loosening tka without attending-level implant, fixation, or infection context.
Attending pearl: connect aseptic loosening tka anatomy, implant interfaces, and shared trauma neighborhoods before operative planning.
Present indication, relevant imaging, implant/fixation concepts, and escalation pathway for aseptic loosening tka.

metadata.trigger: Painful TKA with progressive subsidence and excluded infection
metadata.action: Discuss revision TKA with attending including constraint and bone loss
